description: Per-playbook section configuration for the EIAA standard form v1.0.0 (issue #209). This is the authoritative, hand-curated list of sections in the canonical Exos ContractToaster standard form v1.0.0, including the section 10 sub-clause splitting that cannot be inferred from a generic heading-level parse of the .docx (all four section 10 sub-clauses appear under a single '10. Miscellaneous' heading in the document, but must map to four distinct anchors so the four section 10 topics don't collide in the playbook). Read at runtime by scripts/build_anchor_map.py via the playbook registry (scripts/playbook_registry.py) -- this is DATA, not a Python literal, so a new contract type is added by authoring a new file like this one plus a registry.json entry, with no code edit.

synthetic_text_supplements.sec-8: Neither party shall be liable to the other for consequential damages.

coverage_exempt_rationales.sec-preamble: Preamble identifies the parties and agreement purpose. It carries no reviewable legal clause — counterparty changes here are de minimis (name/address/date corrections). No topic is needed.
coverage_exempt_rationales.sec-signature: Signature block identifies signatories and execution date. It carries no reviewable legal clause — counterparty changes are de minimis. No topic is needed.
coverage_exempt_rationales.sec-1: Section 1 ('Student Training Program') is a structural parent heading that groups §1.1–§1.7. It contains no standalone reviewable legal clause — all substantive obligations are expressed in the numbered sub-sections, each of which has a dedicated topic. No additional topic is needed for the parent heading.
coverage_exempt_rationales.sec-1.1: Section 1.1 ('General Obligations') is an introductory subsection that summarises the §1 block at a high level. It does not introduce obligations beyond those detailed in §1.2–§1.7, which are individually covered by playbook topics. No additional topic is needed.
coverage_exempt_rationales.sec-2.2: Section 2.2 ('Termination') is a structural parent heading that groups §2.2.1 and §2.2.2. It contains no standalone reviewable legal clause — all substantive termination mechanics are in the numbered sub-sections. No additional topic is needed for the parent heading.
coverage_exempt_rationales.sec-2.2.1: Section 2.2.1 ('Without Cause') is absent from the Exos standard form v1.0.0: Exos does not grant a mutual convenience-termination right in this agreement type. The anchor is registered in the form map to ensure the coverage gate flags any counterparty attempt to introduce such a clause; reviewers must create a REQUEST_CHANGE response if this section appears in a redline. Because the clause is intentionally absent, no standing topic is pre-loaded.

sec-10.description: Issue #200: in the real (and SYNTHETIC placeholder) .docx, all four §10 sub-clauses live under ONE literal document heading ('Miscellaneous') -- there is no separate heading per sub-clause for the loader to match by heading text alone. 'source_heading' is that single real heading; 'splits' gives the ordered, document-order list of (anchor, marker) pairs the real-docx loader (scripts/diff_standard_form.py::_load_standard_form_paragraphs_from_docx) uses to partition the body paragraphs following that heading into the four sub-clause anchors, by matching each paragraph's leading lettered-paragraph marker. A paragraph with no marker is treated as a continuation of whichever split most recently matched.
